Summary: DBAPI: Some db code may be called unnecessarily
Description:
(1) On startup, dbstate calls make_database for an in memory database. This does a lot of work, which seems completely unnecessary, especially because a proper database is normally opened next.

(2) Make_database for in memory of DBAPI databases makes many calls to txn.py to instantiate a DbTxn. This is completely unnecessary, because the transaction does not appear to be started.

(3) When a DBAPI database is started, make_database is called twice, which is not necessary.

All of this makes Gramps startup much slower than is necessary. The attached daignostic output shows timing.
Steps To Reproduce: See script in the attached trace. (CLI create database and imports a .gramps file into it).
Additional Information: See attached diagnostic output (trace.txt)

Summary: refactor GUI tools to use User and enable CLI operation
Description:
The gen.user.User() class should be added to tools also,
now that it is in reports.
Additional Information: Summary so far: (done) 1) Replace the currently unused User.prompt stub with a method based on QuestionDialog2 interface instead ({gen|cli|gui}.User) (done) 2) Add a new cmdline switch -y --yes for cmdline unattended execution of cli.User.prompt(), that would force the 1st (default) answer w/o asking the user (done) 3) Also (lower prio.) add a new cmdline switch -q --quiet to suppress extra diagnostic during unattended runs, such as progress indicator. Back-merged to gramps40 up to this point. (done) 4) Add uistate to gui User (done) 5) Unify cli and gui tool classes' __init__ signature, using user in place of uistate in the current gui tools' __init__. Make the running of cli/gui tools use keyword arguments only, so that future API upgrades are easier! NOTE: sortevents inherits __init__ from ToolManagedWindowBatch, change there, too. (done) 6) Change uistate to user in BatchTool sig, (not done - do we need it?) make it the last positional arg (done) 6.5) update in gramps-addons -- some trunk tests are broken because climerge needs an update! Consider back-merging at this stage? ((done) - also on gramps40) 6.7) Implemented User.progress - a context manager wrapper around begin_progress/step_progress/end_progress. ((done) - also on gramps40) 6.8) Convert the existing code using progress meter to use user.progress (implemented in #7061) instead. This would also eliminate the ugly stale progress meter dialogs that Gramps likes leaving around when something crashes. 7) Then, as a second stage (trunk only!), we can convert the legacy tools from code conditioned on uistate and directly using the UI dialogs/progress meter/binary choices to code delegating to self.user. Wherever interactive dialogs beyond those expressed with user.prompt exist to set operation modes, we should use relevant options settable from command line to provide proper defaults. Nick's summary of tool conversion TODO: >> >> I started to look at the current GUI-only tools in core Gramps. >> >> 1. Tools that should be able to run from the command line: >> (done) >> Extract Event Description >> Merge Citations >> Sorts events >> >> 2. Tools that would be better as gramplets: >> (done) >> Interactive Descendant Browser >> Relationship Calculator (done) >> Generate SoundEx Codes >> >> 3. Tools that require selection from a list: >> >> Fix Capitalization of Family Names >> Extract Information from Names >> Extract Place Data from a Place Title >> Remove Unused Objects >> Not Related >> >> 4. Other tools with a GUI interface: >> (done) >> Python Evaluation Window (debug) (done) >> Show Uncollected Objects (debug) >> Compare Individual Events >> Find Possible Duplicate People >> Media Manager >> Edit Database Owner Information > Looks good. > The last one sounds like something that could be of use in > cmdline-only mode, to set database owner info prior to an export batch > job. I suggest you make your change in trunk. Then we can start to convert most tools to run from the command line. We can think about the GUI tools in section 4 of my list later. Maybe they belong in a different category of plugin? Nick. -- The eventual goal would be to remove the import (and use) of ProgressMeter from tools which can be run from a CLI. (See trunk rev 18378 for when User() was put into reports, and trunk rev 18388 for when the ProgressMeter was removed.)

Summary: Dates are not sorted consistently
Description:
This probably has been discussed before, but I really feel that this is a bug... even if this is a python issue, can't programmers create a workaround so that dates are sorted consistently.

This means that all dates with just the year should be grouped (preferably before all the detailed dates and after all the dates with a pre-alphabet descriptor.

Then all the dates with pre-descriptors should be grouped together (IE all "about YYYY" grouped together, all "before YYYY" grouped together, all "after YYYY" grouped together, etc)f

I'm not too concerned about the order of the between, after, before, about, etc... but I do want them grouped together.

I've attached an example of what I have to deal with when I sort the column by birthdate in the person view.
Steps To Reproduce:
Select "People Category view". 

Sort the columns by birthdate

Summary: [Manage Family Trees] dialog does not re-focus on selected tree after Sort change
Description: The initial sorting order in the Manage Family Trees dialog is alpha-sort ascending. Choosing a different sort order keeps the dialog focused on the Row number visible in the dialog previous to the sort. It should re-focus on the Selected tree's row number in the newly sorted list.
Steps To Reproduce:
Assume that the dialog has 40 alpha-sorted rows, showing rows 4-13 of that list with row 8 selected.

After changing from Ascending sort to Descending sort, the dialog still shows rows 4-13 ... but with a different sorting for the list of records.  And the same record is selected but out-of-view at the 8th row from the bottom ... so you have to page down to find it.  (or press the up arrow or down arrow key... that re-focuses the dialog)

Summary: Frequent spurious "Place cycle detected" errors when opening a Place
Description:
When I open a Place in the Places view I frequently get a "Place cycle detected" error even though there is no place cycle/location loop.

I began a Gramps file by importing a GEDCOM, so I started with a flat list of place names. I am a Gramps newbie.

When I open places I fairly often will get "Place cycle detected". If I inspect the "Enclosed by" places for that place, there is no loop. Sometimes it happens for Places that aren't Enclosed By anything. I've tried rebuilding the database and sometimes that seems to work for a place, but not always, I don't think it's having any effect, I think that it is somewhat random if I get the error or not. I've exported my gramps to XML and re-imported and that makes no difference.
Steps To Reproduce: It's inconsistent. All I have to do is open a Place for editing and the error may or may not appear. I've attached a screenshot showing one instance where I've opened Raglan Township, Harrison County, Iowa for the first time ever and it showed the error , despite having no "Enclosed by" places. This is *VERY* *aggravating*.  I've had Gramps open the same link half a dozen times in a row quick one after another.
Steps To Reproduce:
Select the Note Category
Create a new note
Paste the following example link
http://files.usgwarchives.net/la/orleans/vitals/births/index/1892bifh.txt
Select Ok

Reopen that note
Click in the area beside(but not on) the link or below

Summary: [Navigator-Charts-Pedigree-View] Image doesn't fit frame in chart
Description: I noticed when setting the tree size to 6 or more that some of the images do not fit the frame in the chart (see attached screenshot). This happens in the last visible generation that has images. I used equally sized images, so this isn't caused by a difference in dimensions.
Steps To Reproduce:
Add a few (equally sized) images to each of six or seven generation in your tree.
Set tree size to 6 or 7.
Additional Information: I suspect it may have something with long names or dates (For example "Estimated between 1880 and 1890") in another person of the same generation. I.e. two different great-great grandmothers - one with an image and the other with an exceptionally long name or birth/death date (but no picture).

Summary: descendant chart does not scale text down in ODT output
Description:
A (graphic) descendant chart report does not scale down the
text in ODT output.  PDF output is fine, by comparison.
Steps To Reproduce:
Generate a (graphic) descendant chart, in ODT, say in a
letter/portrait size, with at least three generations (more
is better), and on the "Size" tab pick either of the "scale
tree to fit" options, then look at the result, for instance
with OpenOffice's oowriter or LibreOffice's equivalent.  The
boxes are fine but the text is not scaled down.
Additional Information:
This problem was in 3.3.0 too but as that gramps branch will
not have any future releases I am filing this against 3.4.x.

Summary: Family Sheet text-report: Reports place notes and locked notes, repeatedly
Description:
In the Family Sheet text-report, you can choose to include or not include notes in the report. However, if you choose to include the notes, that will mean that you also includes notes from the PLACES where this families lived. Not once, but every time a place is mentioned. This also includes place notes that have been locked (i.e. that I have locked in order NOT to have them included in any reports). As every family member (at least parents and children) in most cases have lived or have events that have taken place in the same place, the place notes text will swell to an almost insane amount. Therefore, I would rather call this a bug-report than a feature request.

Currently, one can only decide if one wants to include or not include notes. Of course one wants to include the relevant notes about the people, but the place information is hardly of interest, especially not when it is given in this multi-repeat way. 

-It would be desirable to be able to exclude place notes from the Family Sheet text-report. 
- It would be desirable to be able to exluce notes that are locked.
Additional Information: https://www.gramps-project.org/wiki/index.php/Family_Sheet

Summary: Inconsistent final punctuation of heading & text paragraphs in reports [at least DAR, DDR, DDRI]
Description:
Some of the text reports generated by gramps produce output with inconsistent and inappropriate final punctuation of headings, and also with inconsistent final punctuation of text.

A text object that functions as a heading should not have trailing punctuation [such as a full-stop or colon], at least in the world of professional, academic & government publishing I have worked in.

In text reports from gramps 4.2.8-3 and earlier, some headings finish with a full stop, others with a colon, and most others [correctly] with no punctuation mark at all. To make matters worse, some have the superscript endnote reference numbers [if any] prior to a punctuation mark, others have it after. The sequence of the superscript endnote references and the punctuation mark will cease to be an issue if the erroneous punctuation is removed.

The text output from at least the Detailed Ancestral Report [DAR], Detailed Descendant Report [DDR], and Detailed Descendant Report with Images [DDRI] is affected, but there may be other report types I have not yet noticed that also exhibit these errors & inconsistencies.

The three reports referred to all use an elegant scheme of writing text to paragraphs tagged to a paragraph style for seamless integration into any software that handles Open Document Text [odt], and also supports indexing and many other useful publication tools. The same styles are also used to generate other file formats like pdf, so the default settings are not irrelevant. While the default styles have some minor shortcomings, the great benefit of this approach is that a user can load their own style sheet which sets  every aspect of document formatting they may wish to tweak, literally with just a few mouseclicks—quickly and easily overwriting the new report text with the user's settings for headers, footers, font sizes, weights, colours, para indents, spacings, keep-with-next, non-break settings and so on. So this is a great production workflow tool.

When writing to odt, the current code from the above three reports, when creating HEADING paragraphs, produces at least four different forms of output structure [where underscores indicate spaces, numbers indicate superscript endnote refs]:

(1) FAMNAME,_Givens4,_7._¶

(2) More_about_FAMNAME,_Givens:¶

(3) Spouse:_FAMNAME,_Givens._4,_7¶ or Relationship_with:_FAMNAME,_Givens._4,_7¶

(4) Children_of_X_and_Y¶

Note that (1) has endnote_refs_if_any+full-stop+trailing_space;  (2) has final colon; (3) has full-stop+endnote_refs_if_any+trailing space; and (4) does not use a punctuation mark at all [which is how all headings should be by default]! It should also be noted that the position of the endnote superscripts is inconsistent between (1) and (3).

When creating TEXT paragraphs, the reports are also inconsistent as to how the paragraphs are finished off. In some cases, particularly the first text paragraph for any person, there is usually the contents+endnote_refs_ifany+full-stop+trailing_space [which means users have to learn to leave a final full-stop OUT of the description field in events, to avoid two successive full-stops getting printed! If they do include a final full-stop within the description, and there are also citations, the text will print with a full-stop [theirs], then the superscript endnote reference(s), then another full-stop, then a trailing space]. In other words, these text paragraphs get the same treatment as (1) above. But other text paragraphs may be structured in one of two [inconsistent] ways, depending on the origin of the material—(a) descriptions to events subsequent to the first for any person appear to be structured as in (1), with endnote_refs_ifany+full-stop+trailing_space always added onto the existing content; or (b) notes of any type always seem to be structured with their contents only, with no additional stops or trailing spaces [so the user DOES need to remember to finish any note with a full-stop, unless it is not a complete sentence anyway!] In other words, these are, correctly, treated like (4).

Numbered lists within the text [i.e. the children within families] are treated as for (1).

The main exception to the pattern of text treatment noticed so far, is that if an individual has an alternative name entry [which usually? print as first paragraph in the "More about..."  section], it prints as e.g. "Also known as: Johnny_" with a trailing space but no full stop. This may be yet another text structuring variant (5?) buried within the code.

The trailing space in (1) & (5?) is pretty redundant. It probably doen't cause grief much of the time, but it should be noted that it will sometimes screw up justification or kerning if a user has those turned on.

Before a fix for these errors & inconsistencies is planned, it might also be helpful to contemplate an update to the naming schema of paragraph styles, particularly to make more explicit those that function as headings, to distinguish them from those that function as other parts of a publication. If the schema is updated, it might be helpful to make it consistent across all reports that use it.

Referring for the moment to the existing schema of paragraph style names, the main heading problems relate to the paragraphs that are tagged as:

*-First-Entry—which despite its name is in fact used only as the main heading for every individual listed in a report]; it is currently given treatment (1)

*-MoreHeader—which is used in two different contexts, headings of the form "More about..." get treatment (2); headings of the form "Spouse..." or "Relationship with..." get treatment (3)

*-NoteHeader—which [inconsistently] get treatment (2) for notes on families [so the problem is the minority case], but (4) for notes on individuals [so no problem in what is the majority case]

The code that writes the *-First-Entry paragraphs appears to share code with the variant of text writing that unilaterally appends endnote_refs_if_any+full-stop+trailing_space. I do not have sufficient knowledge of python and have not succeeded in fixing it, as it looks like there could be unintended consequences of an attempted fix. Similarly, I have not managed to identify how the issue of inconsistent treatment of text paragraphs is caused or might be fixed.

The code that appends the colon onto the content of *-MoreHeader & *-NoteHeader is fairly easy to locate, and omitting the colon from the relevant section [in the 4.2.8-3 version of detdescendantreport.py, line 679 for DDR-NoteHeader in the family case, plus six successive instances  at 702, 721, 805, 824, 835, 860 for DDR-MoreHeader] fixes those headings.
Steps To Reproduce:
Run any of the Detailed Ancestor Report, Detailed Descendant Report or Detailed Descendant Report with all Images, preferably from a point high up in a fairly large and rich tree, as some instances [such as notes on families] may not be evident in many reports.

If the resultant odt is then loaded, searching to the offending  paragraph styles will quickly illustrate the problem, but variant (1) will be prominent within the first few lines in most reports.

Summary: Dynamic Web Report (DWR) broken links from a custom page to media (unless object also referenced from individual or family)
Description:
In a Dynamic Web Report (DWR), if the creator includes a custom page containing a link to a media object, the reference in the resultant web pages is defective unless the same media object is also referred to from one of the individuals or families included in the DWR.

For example, if a custom page contains a link to a media object that is also referenced by an individual or family listed in that DWR, the relevant custom page html code includes <a href="media.html?mdx=0">, where the mdx value is in this case to the gramps example database media object O00000, and the link is valid. But if a custom page reference is to a validly added media object in the same database (as O00999 in my test), but which is referenced ONLY from the custom text page, it appears in the html as <a href="media.html"> with no ID reference, and (unsurprisingly) the link does not work, and the supposedly-linked media object is not copied into the DWR image folders.
Steps To Reproduce:
1. Load the gramps example database (and verify in media view that its media files are in position and are viewable).

2. Add some distinctive new media object into the media view, giving it at least a title.

3. Create a note containing some text which refers in some appropriate way to:

(a) any one of the original media objects of the example database, using the link editor to select some or all of the relevant text and create a valid hotlink from that text to that relevant media object; and 

(b) the distinctive new media object, also using the link editor to create a valid link to that actual object.

4. Create a DWR of the entire d/b, including the note created at step 3 as the text for one of the available custom pages, and including images and media.

5. Examine the media index and note that the media object referred to at 2 and 3(b) is not listed.

6. Click on the tab for the custom page containing the test links.

7. Click on the link for the image that was already included in the example database, and note that the link is valid.

8. Click on the link for the distinctive new media object and note that the link is broken.

In the screenshot attached, the link to the first works, but to the second is broken. In media view, the path to the supposedly-linked file for the second hotlink is verified, and the file can be viewed, but the link in the DWR is just to <a href="media.html"> and the media object file has not copied into the DWR image folders.
Additional Information:
Until this bug is fixed, there is a workaround, after creation of the DWR:

1. copy the missing media object into the image folder of the DWR (top level will do, it does not need to be in a sub-folder)

2. edit the relevant custom_x.html file and replace the hotlink code

<a href="media.html">

with 

<a href="image\FILENAME.EXT">

(where FILENAME.EXT is the relevant filename of the media object copied into the image folder)

Summary: [RCS - Archive ] Tree Manager can't rename archived backup [  TypeError: sequence item 0: expected str instance, bytes found ]
Description:
When I tried to rename an archived backup of a family tree, I got the following error:

2024-12-01 07:30:35.950: ERROR: grampsapp.py: line 188: Unhandled exception
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python3.12/site-packages/gramps/gui/dbman.py", line 614, in __change_name
    self.__rename_revision(path, new_text)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.12/site-packages/gramps/gui/dbman.py", line 638, in __rename_revision
    message = "\n".join(proc.stderr.readlines())
              ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
TypeError: sequence item 0: expected str instance, bytes found

Seems to be a missing ".decode()" on each element of the list returned by readlines.
Steps To Reproduce:
1. Launch Gramps
2. Open the Tree manager window (if not showing at startup)
3. Click on "Rename" a backup of a family tree

The previous error shows up.
Additional Information:
I managed to fixed this issue using the following patch. However I saw in the dbman.py code that other code lines similar to this may crash at running time with the same error:
$ grep -n "readlines())" dbman.py
638:        message = "\n".join(proc.stderr.readlines())
810:        message = "\n".join(proc.stderr.readlines())
1180:    message = "\n".join(proc.stderr.readlines())
1225:        message = "\n".join(proc.stderr.readlines())
1256:    message = "\n".join(proc.stderr.readlines())

Patch:
--- gramps/gui/dbman.py	2024-12-01 07:44:15.944113096 +0100
+++ gramps/gui/dbman_fixed.py	2024-12-01 07:45:07.675990385 +0100
@@ -635,7 +635,7 @@
 
         proc = subprocess.Popen(cmd, stderr=subprocess.PIPE)
         status = proc.wait()
-        message = "\n".join(proc.stderr.readlines())
+        message = "\n".join((l.decode() for l in proc.stderr.readlines()))
         proc.stderr.close()
         del proc

Summary: [D3 Chart Addon] Update of d3.js and jquery libraries to newer versions
Description:
Can someone please update the D3.js and Jquery.js libraries for the 3rd party add-on "D3 Charts"
This Libraries are 5-6 years old.

It seem that these old libraries has some kind of memory leak, in addition, there can be and has been multiple secirity issues by running that old javascript libraries...
Additional Information:
[copied from wiki]
D3 Chart update of d3.js and jquery libraries to newer versions
* Uses D3.js version 3.4.5 (and not the current release) https://d3js.org/  https://github.com/d3/d3/releases
* Uses jquery v1.10.2 and 2.0.3 which are no longer supported or patched  https://jquery.com/  https://github.com/jquery/jquery/releases
* Uses jquery-ui v1.10.4 which is also outdated https://jqueryui.com/ https://github.com/jquery/jquery-ui

Does not work with MS-IE 11?[2] If anyone is still facing the problem [with MS-IE 11] here the solution is to add these 2 lines to your html file:

<meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=edge" />

If not IE doesn't understand the property CSSStyleDeclaration in your d3.js file. 

https://github.com/gramps-project/addons-source/tree/maintenance/gramps60/D3Charts/js

https://gramps-project.org/wiki/index.php/Addon:D3_Ancestral_and_Descendant_Charts

Summary: [MacOS ]View configuration: Column editor duplicates fields when using drag-n-drop to reorder columns
Description: [on MacOS ]When using the Column Editor to configure a view such as People view, a drag-and-drop operation to reposition a field e.g. ID, actually duplicates the field instead of moving it. This can be repeated over and over, creating many entries which cannot be removed via the UI.
Steps To Reproduce:
1. Open a list view such as People view
2. Choose View > Configure...
3. Select the ID field and drag it to another location

Expected: ID field moves from its current position to the new location
Actual: ID field is duplicated so that there two copies of the field in the view

This can be repeated over and over and the duplicate field(s) cannot be removed via the UI.
Additional Information:
Workaround:
Navigate to the view's configuration file in the local file system e.g. People_personview.ini and manually edit 'rank' and 'size' arrays to remove the duplicates.

see Discourse report:

https://gramps.discourse.group/t/does-citation-tree-view-mode-allow-citation-deletion/7798

Summary: [Grouped People views] Quick View menu items selectable even when items cannot be applied, [QuickView Error: handle is not set]
Description:
Quick View from the context menu in People > Grouped View shows all items under it as selectable even when a surname item (as opposed to a person) is selected, and then fails with this message in the console:

QuickView Error: handle is not set

Unless the user is actively monitoring the console window, they would not know that the operation failed and perhaps try again with the same results. Ideally menu items that cannot be applied to the selection would be grayed out/unselectable, or if the operation can be applied to all items under the selected last name then it should apply to all.
Steps To Reproduce:
1. Open any family tree, e.g. Gramps sample
2. Go to People view, and set it to Grouped People so that Last names are shown
3. Select any last name (not a person) and right click to invoke context menu
4. Select any item from the Quick View submenu, e.g. All Events, Biography etc.

Observe that no action is taken, and if you look at the console window, this error message is shown: QuickView Error: handle is not set. As expected, menu items work correctly when an individual is selected.
Additional Information: Compare this behavior to Quick View in Places > Grouped Places view. If a country is selected then Quick View > Place references applies to all items below the country.

Summary: typo in source comments (Sphinx dev docs cleanup) (  "sence" >> "sense")
Description: typo: "sence" {sec, recte "sense")
Steps To Reproduce:
https://github.com/gramps-project/gramps/blob/96f2b6bc04fa248dca85e15763efdb513c9db0ed/gramps/gen/utils/unknown.py#L91

    formally correct we would need to specify a interface (in the OOP sence)
Additional Information:
The paragraph introducing the "make_unknown" is neither clean nor crisp.  It could use a rewrite

Original:   https://github.com/gramps-project/gramps/blob/96f2b6bc04fa248dca85e15763efdb513c9db0ed/gramps/gen/utils/unknown.py#L85-L94

>    Some theoretical underpinning
>    This function exploits the fact that all import methods basically do the
>    same thing: Create an object of the right type, fill it with some
>    attributes, store it in the database. This function does the same, so
>    the observation is why not use the creation and storage methods that the
>    import routines use themselves, that makes nice reuse of code. To do this
>    formally correct we would need to specify a interface (in the OOP sence)
>    which the import methods would need to implement. For now, that is deemed
>    too restrictive and here we just slip through because of the similarity in
>    code of both GEDCOM and XML import methods.

possible rewrite: (needs validation by a programmer. target audience is novice to writing Gramps Importer plugins)

    Theoretical Underpinnings of the "make_unknown" function
    -------------------------
    This function is designed to mirror how data import routines work: they 
    create an object, set its attributes, and then save it to the database. 
    By using the same creation and storage methods as the import routines, 
    we avoid duplicating code and ensure consistency.

    Ideally, we would define a formal interface (in the object-oriented 
    programming sense) that all import methods would implement. This 
    would enforce a consistent structure and make the code easier to 
    maintain. 

    However, since both the GEDCOM and XML import methods are 
    already quite similar, we can take advantage of this similarity and 
    reuse their logic directly, without introducing strict interfaces for now.

Summary: 'Estimated' in Age Calculator is contaminated by 'about' approximation limits
Description:
ages for explicitly limited date ranges still use the Preference limits for approximating.  An explicit approximation using a "between" should override the Preferences.  

e.g., even though the 'about' limit is "±50 years" by default, a "±5 years" approximation can be be explicitly defined with "estimated between 1960 and 1970". This should not be reinterpreted as "estimated between about 1960 and about 1970" which calculates as "from 1910 to 2020". (Note that pinning 'Estimated' on the "±5 years" results is still worthwhile.)

A person, born in 1936, has a relationship with an other person (birthdate unknown). From memory I only know vague dates. So in the Person category I made an event ‘Relationship’ with the following date entry ‘estimated between 1968 and 1978’. Which is accepted as being valid. When I close the date entry, the age of the person is calculated 'between 19 years and 94 years).
Steps To Reproduce: always with estimated date
Additional Information:
Going into  preferences > Limits
verifies that
Setting the Date 'about' year range to a lower value lowers the result of the estimated age range in person view

is this an error of Gramps 6.0.0 core or of the "Verify the data" tool?

Summary: Reports->Graph->Family Lines Graph removes direct line ancesters
Description:
The "Try to remove extra people and families" option removes direct line ancestors.

Originally reported in #10400, this is a much simplified example of the problem.
happens in version 4.2.6 and 5 alpha 3.
Steps To Reproduce:
- create new empty tree
- import attached file
- go to Reports->Graph->Family Lines Graph
- on "Person of Interest" tab select only the person ID I0000, "familyName, person of interest".

produce inclusive report:
- on "Report Options" tab, check mark to follow children and parents
- clear the check mark for "Try to remove extra people and families"
- produce a report with a name like "include.pdf"

produce exclusive report:
- on "Report Options" tab, check mark to follow children and parents
- SET the check mark for "Try to remove extra people and families"
- produce a report with a name like "exclude.pdf"

compare the 2 reports. 

in the exclude report see that direct line ancestors of the person of interest are removed.
Additional Information:
Source of the problem seems to be changes in the spellings of surnames.

Even though the spellings of surnames may change over time, the direct line ancestors should be included in the report.

Summary: Linkrot for GEDCOM55 specification document in plugins/lib/libgedcom.py
Description:
The specification archive referenced at the beginning of plugins/lib/libgedcom.py has been removed from familysearch.org
the link to the "GEDCOM55.EXE" (specification  + samples) archive is no longer valid
Steps To Reproduce:
Look at lines 29-30 on  gramps/plugins/lib/libgedcom.py 
https://github.com/gramps-project/gramps/blob/73c3ec402c91871e49a6b30a86d2f6da86fd921f/gramps/plugins/lib/libgedcom.py#L29-L30

the link to the "GEDCOM55.EXE" is no longer valid

Should it be changed to the 5.0 family specifications at:
https://gedcom.io/specs/#version-5

Also, should it say 5.5.1 instead of 5.5 ? Since GEDCOM 5.5 only supports ASCII and ANSEL encodings and this lib file supports UTF-8 and UTF-16
Additional Information:
Found an Internet Archive capture that has a valid download. (But the Archive Manager on Fedora 37 reports an "error occurs" when opening the file):
https://web.archive.org/web/20060623090929/http://www.familysearch.org/GEDCOM/GEDCOM55.EXE

https://www.bitweaver.org/wiki/GEDCOM says:
GEDCOM55.EXE contains the official version of the standard, and various samples to assist management of the information will be produced here.

Summary: Uninstall of an (manual) add-on Rule destroys the FilterRules folder.
Description: Using uninstall on a single Addon Rule via Plugin Manager Enhanced will uninstall ALL addon Rules and delete the FilterRules folder.
Steps To Reproduce:
Find the plugins/FilterRules folder for Gramps. (If no addon Rules have been installed, this folder does not exist. In that case, Install an addon Rule to Gramps) 
Add a subfolder to the FilterRules folder and add dummy text file so that the folder is not empty. 
Leave the subfolder open on the desktop

Open  Gramps.
Help -> Plugin Manager (I am using the enhanced version)
filter to 'rule'
    several are installed
select 1 "Source matching parameters"
click the 'uninstall' Button
    no rules are 'installed', all show as 'Available'
    the subfolder window is gone from the desktop
    browse back to the plugins folder.  The FilterRules folder, the manually added subfolder and file are GONE

return to Gramps
  install any 'Available' Rule.  All are installed
Additional Information:
I had manually installed the Degrees of Separation filter while it was under development.

Once it was released through the updater, I wasn't sure whether Gramps was seeing the manually installed version or the distributed version.  So I did  an uninstall, intending to reinstall.  I copied the existing filters to a subfolder in the FilterRules.
(for my Windows OS) C:\Users\DistrictSupport\AppData\Roaming\gramps\gramps51\plugins\FilterRules  )
The folder was left open.
During uninstall of a single add-on Rule, the OS closed the FilterRules folder.  It had been deleted.

Note, I had run into a problem with default person (Home Person) crashing Gramps when no Home person is defined. (New Tree with import. No Home set by default) and had saved the Gramps fault report in this folder.  It was lost during the uninstall

Summary: [Top Surnames Gramplet] Quick view lastnames in Dashboard, does not find persons
Description:
In the dashboard there is a list of the top 10 lastnames. If I select one with eg. 3%, it can not find any persons with that last name. This is strange because when there are no persons with that lastname, why are they in the top10 list ?
I guess it is a search-filter problem of the quick-view of lastnames.
Steps To Reproduce:
Start gramps with an empty tree. Import the csv file. Have a look at the dashboard, and you find 5x (3%) persons with the lastname "Boer". Double click on the name, and you will not find anyone.
If you look in "persons" in the left, you can find the persons, so they are in the database.
Additional Information:
The lastnames exist of a prefix and the "real" lastname. Maybe the search filter adds prefix and lastname to one string, and then it can not find "Boer".

In the persons interface, the persons are under "Boer", but every persons has the name "de Boer".

https://www.gramps-project.org/wiki/index.php/Gramps_5.2_Wiki_Manual_-_Gramplets#Top_Surnames

In this thread @ennoborg identified strings related to Berkeley database which shouldn't be present in Gramps 5.2 because support for working with BSDDB has been removed. Nick suggested that some string translation files haven't been updated, so this bug is being filed to update those files.

Summary: [Fan Chart Report]  Font size inconsistent across generations
Description: I experience a problem with varying font size when creating a graphical report of a fan chart. The font size seems to vary across generation, but inconsistently, with sometimes small fonts when there is much more space available in the box. In the attached example, fonts are very small in the sixth (yellow) generation, but much bigger in the 9th generation (orange).  I tried using a custom style with font sizes defined for each generation, but that is not reflected in the exported fan chart. I have added an example. Does someone have a solution, or could explain what I am doing wrong?
Steps To Reproduce:
Export 11 generation ancestor Fan chart graphical report, with these settings:
Paper options:
•	A0, landscape, Margins all over: 0 cm
•	Output format: PDF 
•	Style: customize style fan chart (tried with different variations in font sizes depending on generation, or identical font sizes for all generations)
	
Type of graph: full circle
•	Background colour: generation dependent
•	Orientation of radial texts: upright
•	Draw empty boxes: no
•	Use one style for all generations: no
•	Exclude the title: yes
•	Include data marked private: yes
•	Living people: Included, and all data
•	Translation: Dutch
Additional Information: https://gramps-project.org/wiki/index.php/Gramps_5.2_Wiki_Manual_-_Reports_-_part_4#Fan_Chart

Summary: Fan Chart Report using combination of report options does not produce expected output
Description:
When creating a fan chart report using the following report options
Background color: generation dependent
Selecting "Use one font style for all generations"
The resulting fan chart output has different colors for each generation, as expected, but the font sizes vary (when I expected it to be all the same size, because of the selected "use one font style for all generations"option).

But when creating a fan chart report using the following report options
Background color: generation dependent
Unselecting "Use one font style for all generations"
The resulting fan chart output has different font sizes, but the background color DOES NOT have different colors for each generation.  All generations are white.
Even though there is some variation for the font sizes, for the 4th, 5th and 6th generations, there is space for the font to be larger.
Steps To Reproduce: Generate fan charts with the options described above, and compare the output.

Summary: After GEDCOM import  of CAL dates Gramps date validation does not recognise "Cal" [but does "Calculated"]
Description:
When importing a calculated date represented by
1 BIRT
2 DATE Cal 1847
the date should be handled the same as Abt


------------
GEDCOM import should import "CAL" date fields as "Calculated"
Steps To Reproduce:
*Import the test file
*Select "Tools > Utilities > Verify the Data..."
*Select "Run"

View the results
Additional Information:
http://homepages.rootsweb.ancestry.com/~pmcbride/gedcom/55gcch2.htm#DATE 
DATE_APPROXIMATED: = {Size=4:35}
[
ABT  |
CAL  |
EST 
]
Where :
ABT =About, meaning the date is not exact.
CAL =Calculated mathematically, for example, from an event date and age.
EST =Estimated based on an algorithm using some other event date.
----------------------------------------------- http://wiki-en.genealogy.net/GEDCOM/DATE-Tag#Approximated_Dates 
 Approximated Dates

For approximate dates following options are provided:

n DATE ABT 

or

n DATE CAL 

or

n DATE EST 

Examples:

n DATE ABT 1700

means: The date is not exact, but about 1700. By this no statement is made as far away from 1700 the event took place.

n DATE CAL JUN 1910

means: Calculated mathematically, for example, from an event date and age. From other known facts, it was calculated that the event occurred in June 1910.

n DATE EST 1875

means: Estimated based on an algorithm using some other event date.
----------------------------------------------- https://github.com/gramps-project/gramps/blob/master/gramps/plugins/lib/libgedcom.py#L638

Summary: Jump to Gramps ID functionality does not re-center the view if the target ID is already selected
Description: Entering a ID of a record already selected in the View for a Jump to Gramps ID does nothing for the "Jump to"
Steps To Reproduce:
load the Exmpale.gramps tree
choose  the People flat list (not grouped) view mode
do an extended selection  of several people (just for thoroughness, make it a discontinuous selection)
Note the ID of one of the selected people and a deselected ID in the same screen space
scroll the screen untill the selections are off-screen
open the "Jump to Gramps ID" dialog with the Ctrl-J keybinding
enter the noted ID and click the Jump To button.
    nothing happens in the view and the status bar reports nothing

open the "Jump to Gramps ID" dialog with the Ctrl-J keybinding
enter enter an invalid ID (like "junk"  and click the Jump To button.
    nothing happens in the view but the status bar reports "Error: ____ is not not a valid Gramps ID"

open the "Jump to Gramps ID" dialog with the Ctrl-J keybinding
enter enter the noted deselected ID and click the Jump To button.
    the view re-centers on and selects that noted deselected ID row and deselects the extended selection
Additional Information:
It seems like the view should always re-center (re-focus) on a Jump To a valid ID, even if that is off-center by 1 row.
And the active record in the View should be that focused ID.

However, what should be done about the selection might be something for discussion.  
* Should it change the selection (or extended selection) only if the ID for the Jump To is deselected? 
* Or if the target ID is selected, maybe the (extended) selection should be left as is and just re-center the view and make the record active.

Sometimes it would be useful to continue a complex extended selection. So maybe a feature request for a checkbox in the Jump To dialog in the dialog for "change selection"?

Summary: Windows (NTFS) with db security setup to 'Deny' write permissions not detected as read-only
Description:
I get a popup;
[Errno 13] Permission denied: 'C:\\Users\\prc\\AppData\\Roaming\\gramps\\grampsdb\\59e270c4\\lock'

I think this should be detected as read-only and treated as such.

Issue applies to both bsddb and sqlite dbs.
Steps To Reproduce:
On Windows with Gramps installed on NTFS drive (normal for drive C:) use the file manager to set permissions for the appropriate 'C:\Users\prc\AppData\Roaming\gramps\grampsdb\59e270c4' or similar to 'Deny' writes.  See attached image.

Try to open the db via Gramps.
Additional Information:
Gramps uses the Python os.access(name, os.W_OK) to check for read-only; as noted in the Python documentation:
"Note:  I/O operations may fail even when access() indicates that they would succeed, particularly for operations on network filesystems which may have permissions semantics beyond the usual POSIX permission-bit model. "

The only simple way to actually check for read-only is to try to open a file for write.

Its not clear to me that this is worth the trouble to fix; using Gramps in read-only mode is clearly not often done and  Windows has two ways of setting up a file/directory to be read-only.  The aforementioned permissions and the 'read-only access bits, a legacy from MSDOS days.  The latter is correctly detected by Python .

Summary: "Children" section of the "Families" view needs refresh
Description:
Load the attached Simpson gramps file.
Go to "Families" view.
You are supposed to see four families: from F0001 to F0004.
Select with mouse the family of Bouvier Clancy (the third).
In the bottom of the page you are supposed to see three children from the selected family.
If you can not see the described children, choose "Children" from the tab of the bottom page frame.
Now, in the filter section (on the right of the page) write "Simpson" in the "Father" field and press "Find".
On the main region of the page you are supposed to see only three family and none of them selected.
In spite of this in the bottom part of the page the children of the family of Bouvier Clancy are still displayed.
IMHO that regione should be refreshed.
Steps To Reproduce: See Description

Summary: Add gramplet pop-up does not show arrows for scrolling
Description: When I have a lot of add-ons, the Add Gramplet pop-up is not large enough to show all Gramplets. And on Windows 10, it will then show arrows for scrolling. These arrows are missing on Linux.
Steps To Reproduce: Download a lot (or all) add-ons from the preferences dialog, and then try to add a Gramplet to the right of the person view. The pop-up will then probably too long for a laptop screen like mine (900 pixels vertical) and there are no scroll arrows at the top or bottom of the pop-up.

Summary: [MacOS]Citation Tree View has duplicate Source: Abbreviation columns
Description: Wanted to add the "Source: Abbreviation" (src:abbr) as a column in the Citation Tree View.  Somehow I ended up with multiple src:abbe columns, 5 in fact. Not exactly sure what were the exact steps I did.  In addition to clicking on the src:abbr I was also recording the columns.  Since I have multiple src:abbr columns in the list I can't delete them and start over.
Steps To Reproduce: Went into the "Configure Sources - Citation Tree View - Gramps" option dialog.  I know that I checked the "Source Abbreviation" (src:abbr) checkbox and also drag/dropped it up in the list.  I don't recall if I checked first or dragged first.  I probably dragged a few times and went out to see what the "Citation Tree View" looked like.  Since I'm new to Gramps, I don't know where the configuration/settings are for this option to remove the duplicate src:abbr from the dialog.  This should be one of those items which can never happen but it has for me.  If I could remove this duplicates I could try and recreate the problem, but I don't know how short of reinstalling.

Summary: [Graphviz] Cannot create family trees with thumbnails if user profile path contains non-ascii characters
Description:
I am attempting to generate a family tree pdf that includes thumbnail pictures and am encountering issues.

My home directory contains non-ascii characters (German name with the letter ä): i.e. "C:\Users\Gesine Wxxxxxädt\". Somehow on the command line gramps uses to generate the report this gets mangled to "C:\Users\Gesine Wxxxxx├ñdt\".

As a result the thumbnails that are saved in the user's profile are not found and are not included.
Steps To Reproduce:
1. Have a Windows user name with a non-ascii character
2. Create a person with an associated thumbnail
3. Attempt to render a pdf family tree with thumbnails.
4. Watch the error message in the command line window

Summary: Report - Fan chart - [some] year interpretation[s are] wrong [if calendar is changed to "Persian".]
Description: This failure only occours in the fan chart
Additional Information: Used to be OK en earlier version.

Summary: Graphical Reports, Fan Chart, Print to PDF - Text Orientation Issue - option "Orientation of radial texts" not working
Description:
The "Orientation of radial texts" option offers the choice of "Upright" or "Roundabout".  When directing the output to a PDF format file, the Fan Chart is printed but the text orientation remains the same irrespective of which option is selected.

It always looks like "Roundabout" to me - the text at the bottom of the fan chart is almost upside down. 

If the "Charts" option is selected from the main menu and the Fan Chart is viewed on screen, the text is displayed correctly,
Steps To Reproduce: Select Reports / Graphical Reports / Fan Chart then select PDF as the output format.  Changing the "Orientation of radial texts" option makes no difference to the resulting PDF file.

Summary: Geography view Edit/Print has very small result on Windows 10, independent of paper size
Description:
Trying to print a chart to pdf (or a printer) from chart view.  Result shows the chart on a very small portion of the page, not a full page as I expect.

Seems to be Windows specific as the chart is OK on Linux.

If a different paper size is chosen you may also get a crash.
Steps To Reproduce:
For small size, accept defaults for first dialog.
With second print dialog, select 'Microsoft Print to PDF'.
Accept the defaults, and hit 'Print' button.
Enter File name when that dialog appears.
Examine results.

For crash, accept defaults for first dialog;
With second print dialog, select 'Microsoft Print to PDF'.
Hit 'preferences' button. 
hit 'Advanced' button.
Select 'Tabloid' paper size (or other, non-letter/A4 size).
complete dialogs and crash.
Additional Information:
Seems to be a Gtk or perhaps 'C' library issue for both.
During debug, I provided a fix for small size (moved scaling to the rendering step, after paper size was determined).  But yet another issue appeared; the underlying libraries (gtk or 'C', not sure yet) get the 'Portrait'/'Landscape' messed up when non-default paper sizes are selected, at least for pdf outputs.  The renderer context reports one orientation, but the actual paper orientation of the result is opposite.  So the resulting print is either too small or too large for the page.

Summary: 11 generation fan chart has the text misaligned
Description: 11 generation fan chart has the text misaligned. See attached screen shot. I am willing to help restest this bug if it is fixed.
Additional Information: https://gramps-project.org/wiki/index.php?title=Gramps_4.2_Wiki_Manual_-_Reports_-_part_4#Fan_Chart

Summary: Import of files into Gramps (all Version) only from local files on Windows(xp,7,8,10)
Description: The file chooser pop up windows allow only select local files. A database or file on a network share can not be selected.
Steps To Reproduce: On every Windows platform with network share located files
Additional Information: Using in gramps code of the windows api of file chooser would solve this issue

Summary: [Graph View]Duplication when first cousins married
Description:
Program attempts to show married cousin's together. When there are few people  in the tree, this works greatly. But, when there are alot of people in the tree (like 500), this drawing method ruins the graph. 

Online family tree software of www.myheritage.com, simply show them twice and put a small note on these people that says they are show twice. 

In back end, they are still one person, they are copied. They are just drawn twice.

Summary: [Gramps AIO] Uninstallation deletes files which have not been installed
Description: Gramps uninstallation deletes files and directories that were created before the installation process.
Steps To Reproduce:
Installation by GrampsAIO-4.2.6-1_win64.exe, using all default options, except for the installation directory.
Installation directory is choosen among existing directories, for example in "C:\Program Files\MyPrograms\", which already contains files and directories.
Gramps files are installed directly under "MyPrograms": in this case, no intermediate level like "GrampsAIO64-4.2.6" is created.

When user uninstalls Gramps, all installed files are deleted, plus all files and directories under "MyPrograms", plus directory "MyPrograms\" itself.
Additional Information: Problem is considered "severe", because if a casual user installs Gramps in its main data directory, he will lose all his data (and no backup...).

Summary: Gramps AIO build installers - Should Create a subfolder while Installing to custom location
Description:
I installed gramps to "(Drive):\Program Files" . I was in doubt it will create a folder or start installing Inside "Program Files folder". I had previous version Installed into subfolder, but I couldn't remember it was created by itself or I created it. I thought It would create a subfolder there as other most softwares do. But found that, it is installing in the selected folder. I let it complete the installation thinking to generate the uninstallation script/app. Nor I may need to delete everything one by one later if I intercept in the middle.
But after completing installation, when I run the uninstall.exe, it asked me to select the installation directory, selected "(Drive):\Program Files" where I installed it, and it starts deleting all other folders apps from "(Drive):\Program Files" folder. I immediately stopped it through task manager. But it already deleted several GBs.

It would be nice if gramps create a sub folder in custom installation location.
These things can be considered:
➡ It can create a subfolder named "GrampsAIO64-4.2.6" or "Gramps" in the selected directory.
➡ If the custom selected directory matched with the sub directory which is going to be created automatically while installing, then don't need to create another subfolder inside it. like if I select "(Drive):\Program Files\GrampsAIO64-4.2.6". and Grumps create a subfolder named "GrampsAIO64-4.2.6" during installation, Just simply Install and merge/replace files. Don't Need to create another folder inside it like "GrampsAIO64-4.2.6\GrampsAIO64-4.2.6"
Steps To Reproduce:
➡ Select the Directory  "(Drive):\Program Files" while Installing Gramps.
➡ After completing Installation, Uninstall Through control Panel. or run uninstall.exe
➡ select the installed directory

Summary: Sources And Citation Report shows literal string "researcher name"
Description: When using the "Sources And Citation Report," the default footer field shows the literal string "Copyright 2022 researcher name." It probably is intended to load the researcher name from the Gramps settings and interpret the researcher's name from some variable rather than putting the literal text there.

Assuming Sources and Citation Report is already installed,

Go to Reports -> Text Reports -> Sources and Citations Report

Examine the "Footer" field of the dialog.

Summary: new filters disappear from filter sidebar on switch between views with the filter sidebar
Description:
Apparently the combobox with the filters to be selected in the filter sidebar is initialized separately for the person and person tree views, and so new filters added under person tree view aren't seen (until reload of GRAMPS) in person view.

Each view has to be separately configured to show the general sidebar (gramplet container), and then "person filter gramplet" should be added to it --- both person and person tree view. Once this is done, the issue reproduces.
Steps To Reproduce:
1. Assure you can see the person filter on the sidebar for both person and person tree view 
2. Add new filter(s) under one of the views. Observe the change in the active filter name combobox.
3. Switch to the other view (person tree->person).
Expected: filter list combobox includes the new filter(s).
Actual: it doesn't. If you quit and re-enter gramps, you can see the new filter(s) on all the views.
Additional Information: I believe the person filter gramplet instances should share some state they currently don't. It's likely that other gramplets, too, aren't fully supportive of multiple instances of themselves being used (even in different views) of the same GRAMPS session.

Summary: CSV import does not work with on MS-Windows Gramps AIO if language not installed.
Description:
In 4.2.4 the CSV import had resulted in an Error.
In 4.2.5 the CSV import returns "done", but nothing has been imported.
Steps To Reproduce:
Export a small set of people to CSV
Create a new DB
Import from CSV (it even didn't work with a CSV just containing 1 individual)
Check the "people" tab - it is still empty

Summary: [Tag selection dialog] remove nonfunctional Ctrl-F search textbox interface functionality
Description:
was[Tag selection dialog has Ctrl-F search textbox interface but no functionality]

The interactive search textbox feature for lists is enabled for the Tag Selection dialog but does not scroll the focus of the list.
Steps To Reproduce:
Using the Example.gramps tree
navigate to the People list view
Edit -> Tag -> Organize Tags...
    add enough tags to enable the list scrollbars with a couple pages of options (recommend  26 alphabetical tags  a, b, c, ... x, y, z)
   note the alpha-sorted first & last tag labels.

Edit a person

Minor sub-bug:
  rollover the Tag button 
      hint is "Edit the tag list" but really only opens a dialog to "Edit the tag SELECTIONS", not the options in the "comprehensive list" of Tags.

click the tag button
  an alpha sorted list of tags appears (this is odd since Tags are priority sorted in the other dialogs and that isn't even an option)
  invoke the list search textbox (by typing or pressing Ctrl/Cmd-F) 
  type the last tag label in the list 

Bug: the list does not scroll to the search term.
Additional Information:
The Tag selection editor is confusing for new users in that:
  1) if the tree is New and no Tags exist, the feature offers no options.
  2) the icon is the same as the Toolbar but the behavior is different
  3) the Organize Tags dialog cannot be invoked via this dialog
  4) no color swatches are displayed.
  5) the list is sorted differently (alpha sort) than other Tag lists 

Would it make more sense if it worked EXACTLY the same as the Tag tool on the Toolbar (with a pop-up menu but applied to only the current Object Editor record with no extended selection).

Perhaps the "Tags:" label and the list of currently applied Tags could be hot-linked to invoke the existing Tag Selection GUI

Summary: Selecting more than one object for deletion not consistent, has issues
Description:
If you select more than object, then press the delete button, various issues are shown:

Notes, Media, Repo, Citation, Source, Place, : can't cancel (yes/no only for mass delete); close window and it deletes them all; "no" on mass delete question deletes them all--- should be "Delete all without asking"; after initial question, "cancel" on delete is really "no" because it doesn't cancel asking. Person: can't select option for mass delete, must answer for each. Make like the others.
Steps To Reproduce:

2. press delete button

Summary: [GenealogyTree] LaTeX report includes full images uncropped

Summary: Toolbar overflow indicator not shown
Description:
While testing #12048. Pr # 1274, noticed that the toolbar overflow indicator (down arrow) is not shown until there is a mouse over of the toolbar area or if the Gramps window is covered in some way so it needs to be redrawn.

This issue can be reproduced on debian/linux and macOS, with and without PR # 1274.
Steps To Reproduce:
- Gramps -> Prefrerences -> Display: check the box to "Show text label beside Navigator Buttons"
- restart Gramps
- navigate to the People view
- reduce the size of the Gramps window so that the "Tag" button and title is the last one that will fit in the window.
- there should be a down arrow at the right end of the toolbar
- click the down arrow to see the overflow menu
- change to the alternate view: either "Grouped People" or just "People"
- when window is repainted notice that the overflow indicator/down arrow is absent
- move mouse pointer over the toolbar
- notice that the overflow indicator/down arrow is now present
- reproduce it at will by alternating between the Grouped People" and "People"
Additional Information:
This could be related to issue #9998, which was on Windows, and @prculley created a Partial fix/work around in PR # 369. (applied in 5.0.0-alpha2)

There was a possible issue with GTK functions deprecated since GTK+ 3.10. 

debian has:
    Python 3.9.2 (Passed: version 3.3.0 or greater installed.)
    GTK+ 3.24.24 (Passed: version 3.12.0 or greater installed.)
    PyGObject 3.38.0 (Passed: version 3.12.0 or greater installed.)
    Cairo 1.16.0 (Passed: version 1.13.1 or greater installed.)
    Pycairo 1.16.2 (Passed: version 1.13.3 or greater installed.)
    Pango 1.46.2 (Passed: version 1.29.3 or greater installed.)

Summary: report tables are always written LTR even for RTL locales
Description:
If a report has a "table" then its "rows" are always written
from left to right, even if a locale has been selected whose
text is written from right to left, like Hebrew or Arabic.
Steps To Reproduce:
Start gramps, load in the example.gramps database, then
select the I2106 person.  This is an Arabic woman who was
the second wife of I2110 and they had three children.

Select the Family Group report and choose Arabic as the
output language and PDF as the output format.

Notice the three boxes to the left, under the man, for his
birth, death and marriage "headings", or the "marriage"
heading box all the way to the left under the F0744 family
heading line.

Similarly for the three boxes all the way the left for the
three children.  They are the "1M" and "2M" and "3F" ones.

All those boxes should start the line (they do in English)
and so they should be on the right side of the page.
Additional Information:
Notice the actual text for the birth, death and marriage
headings are correctly written right-to-left (inside the
box, by Cairo or whatever, since they start with Arabic
characters (while the Latin-number dates do not, so they
aren't)).

Summary: [GrampAIO]Outdated version of GTK does not allow for native entry of extended ASCII characters into Windows version of Gramps
Description:
Currently, the Windows installer uses GTK+ 3.18.9, which does not allow for use of the Alt+code entry method for extended ASCII characters. This issue has been closed in GTK 3.24.22 or greater as shown in the following issue tracker: 

https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/gtk/-/merge_requests/991

Could the GTK version used in the Windows installer be updated to use a more recent version that allows for the Alt+code entry of ASCII characters?
Steps To Reproduce: Go into any form field in Gramps and enter an extended ASCII character using the Alt+code method (e.g. hold Alt, then press 132 on the number pad, then release Alt). Upon release, the character should display (ä from the example shown). However, no text is entered currently in Gramps using this method.

Summary: Place Coordinates Gramplet error Unacceptable TLS certificate
Description: When trying to search place, gives an error: The place was not found. g-io-error-quark: Unacceptable TLS certificate (0)

Summary: unsaved data lost when clicking on wrong OK button
Description: Several times I have lost data today, when I clicked on the wrong "OK" button -- when both the family window and the new person window were open.
Steps To Reproduce:
In the relationship view
1) click on the button: "add a new family with person as parent"
2) when the family window appears click on the button" "add a new person as mother"
3) enter name of new person (and other data as needed)
4) THEN, make the mistake to click on the FAMILY window "OK" button (not person "OK" button)
The result is that the person window will close and all newly information in it will be lost
Additional Information: I would expect that either the data would automatically be saved, or a warning dialog pop up, or nothing happen when you click on the family OK button -- but in any case, none of the info in the person window should be lost.

Summary: [CombinedView] Status bar is not updated when Active Person focus changes.
Description:
The Statusbar shows the Active Person in the Relationship in the StatusBar.  But as the Active Person focus is navigated around the family members, the StatusBar name (and relationship if that option is set in Preferences) does not change.

Might be a Windoze issue.

Note that Christopher Horn just corrected a similar StatusBar refresh problem in the Profile relationship view.  But when the refresh was added, the StatusBar showed only the Active Person name and ID, even though the Preferences were set to show the 'Relationship to Home Person' option.

Its there a standard call when changing Active Person focus?  So that Gramps consistently addresses all the required elements?

Summary: Missing zebra lists
Description: In my previous "encarnation" of Gramps, all lists would render with a zebra display (alternating white and gray background). This was good for reading and not to turn listing confuse.
Steps To Reproduce: Open any list (people, places, family).
Additional Information: I don't know whether there is something to do with the Window Manager being used by XFCE4, however Thunderbird displays lists of email messages with zebra without problem.

Summary: [Gramps AIO]Custom installation path =  Installation refers to a non-existent directory and Gramps window never starts
Description:
Please see this reddit post for details: https://www.reddit.com/r/gramps/comments/gy9ue7/grampsaio64_process_hangs_with_error/

In short, choosing a custom path while installing GrampsAIO means that you'll never be able to use the program. I suspect the software has some hardcoded paths ignoring the option given to the user to select a path of their choosing for Gramps.

Some users suggested the dictionaries/languages of the OS/Gramps installation could be the culprits. They aren't. Installing GrampsAIO to the default path ensures it will work regardless of the dictionaries and translations I chose (besides the default English).
Steps To Reproduce:
1. Install GrampsAIO to a custom folder through the standard procedure (editing the offered path during installation). 

2. Execute GrampsAIO.
Additional Information: See the mentioned reddit post for the error messages thrown by Gramps.

Summary: Navigation bar RTL alignment

Summary: DateParserEN failures under the DateTest tool (576/3795)
Description: The partial dates, without a known date but with a known month and year, are displayed OK but not parsed, except the most trivial case of just "Month year" (no modifiers, qualifiers, or slash).
Steps To Reproduce:
1. Run GRAMPS under en_GB.utf-8 locale. Be sure to set LC_ALL explicitly, too, so that dates are displayed/parsed in it, too!
2. Run the Tools/Debug/Check the localized date displayer and parser
3. Observe 576 test failing out of 3795 total.
Additional Information:
The repro is on trunk (it depends on a fix to DateTest tool I have applied today: https://github.com/gramps-project/gramps/commit/180cb1ac28bae3833b6c52100615c9802837c5b6#diff-ba0ecd133d729b50eb134d1f1593dde7 ), but the same parsing code is ancient and predates 3.4

Similar failures happen in other languages' handlers (e.g.: French and Russian), as long as they inherit (by real inheritance or by copy/paste) the relevant parsing from the English date parser.

See also ~31482

Summary: Gramp XML Package (.gpkg) Import loses paths to PNG images
Description:
svn 22176.

This is a rather obscure bug present in all gramps versions since 3.0 or earlier.

On exporting the db as XML and re-importing it into another family tree, all PNG images are lost, although they can be easily retrieved by editing each faulty image and browsing to the correct locations. JPG images have no such problem.

The bug only appears when the media paths are *relative*.
If relative paths are converted to absolute before export, PNG image paths are preserved and retrieved correctly on re-import
Steps To Reproduce:
From the menu select "Family Trees > Make Backup..."

Select "exclude" media and press ok  (This makes a *.gramps XML backup without media)

From the "Family Tree Manager" 
Create a new family tree
From the menu select "Family Trees > Import" and select the *.gramps file you created.

Summary: The [Enter] key performs inconsistantly in Places, Select a Place, Relationships and Surname trees in Group Mode.
Description:
1. People Surname Trees. [Enter] expands and collapses Surname trees in group mode. *

2. In "Select a Place" however, [Enter] neither expands or collapses place trees in group mode, but closes the window and entering which ever place was highlighted at the time in window from which it was called.
 
3. In "Sources" [Enter] neither expands or collapses source trees, but opens the Source description window.  

4. In the "Relationships" "Descendants" Gramplet [Enter] neither expands or collapses Dependent trees and performs no function. 

This is a consistency problem. The user has to remember that [Enter] performs four different ways when in a Group Mode window. 

From a programming standpoint it means two extra subroutine calls for the same key and two extra subroutines creating confusion users. It also adds program maintenance time.

* [To be fair the documentation only lists this [Enter] function only for the Surname trees in group mode.
Steps To Reproduce:
1. Open each window in turn 
* Places, 
* Select a Place, 
* Relationships 
* People 

2. Select Group Mode if not already selected.
3. Highlight a line proceeded by a triangle shaped arrow.
4. Press [Enter].
Additional Information:
The additional functions might be better assigned to different keys reducing confusion in bug reporting resulting in less troubleshooting time for programmers. 

It would cut down on learning time and frustration for the user.

Apparently, Alt+O is cycling focus between the icon for editing the location and the OK button.

- Add event

- Press ok

Summary: website landing page "Fork me on GitHub" ribbon can prevent access to documentation
Description:
The "Fork Me on GitHub" overlay in the upper right of has transparent space where the hotlink can override the critical links on the homepage. The object does not scale with the rest of the page.  This covers "the Documentation" tab link

Summary: Person Editor: Call name is not checked when the given name changes
Description:
When you enter a "Call" name, Gramps checks if that name is contained in the "Given" names and when it's not, marks it red.
Unfortunately, Gramps doesn't check the value of Call when the Given name changes.
Steps To Reproduce:
First example:

- Enter "Jon" as Call Name
--> Given is still empty, so "Jon" is marked red

Enter "Jon" as Given Name
--> "Call" now is correct, but still shown red



Second example:

- Enter "Marc" as Given Name
- Enter "Marc" as Call Name
- Change Given Name to something else
--> "Call" now is incorrect, but still shown black

Summary: Create person: using shortcut for Gallery resets gender
Description:
Whenever I create a new person, either unattached (from People view) or as a child to a family, the gender resets to default when I use the Alt + G + G shortcut to access the Gallery tab.

If Gramps has guessed a gender from the name, it will reset to that gender.
If Gramps couldn't guess a gender, it will reset to unknown gender.

This will not happen when I create a new person as either parent, even if I change the gender from the default it will stay as it is whether I hotkey to the Gallery tab or not.

It will not happen if I browse to the Gallery tab in any other way, including the arrow pad.

I have not observed any other hotkey command resetting the gender info.

At least with this release of Gramps, this has happened every single time regardless of database, hundreds if not thousands of times.
Steps To Reproduce: Create a new person (not as a parent to a family). Set gender. Do anything else. Use Alt + G + G to go to the Gallery tab. The gender has been reset to whatever it was before you changed it.
Additional Information: https://gramps-project.org/wiki/index.php?title=Gramps_4.2_Wiki_Manual_-_Keybindings

Summary: Inconsistent terminology (remove - delete)
Description:
When deleting an object, from people to notes, you select "Remove" from the context menu. 

The following confirmation dialog only talks about "Delete" in the different places (dialog title, title bar, text). 

It should be either "Remove" or "Delete" in both cases.

Summary: Consistency of display order [& appearance of custom entries] in "Drop down list" setting/editing object type
Description:
When setting or editing object types, the list displayed in the drop-down is inconsistently ordered between different categories, and the way custom entries appear is both inconsistent and generally unhelpful.

It would be desirable that display order is always consistently alphabetical.

It would also be desirable that custom entries are either consistently integrated with all others in the sequence, or if separately grouped clearly distinguishable, either by the custom group appearing first, or with a separator marking the change from "standard" categories.
Steps To Reproduce:
This issue relates to the people, families, events, places, repositories & notes categories.

It is most noticeable and problematic for events and places.

While editing any object, selecting the drop-down button for its "Type" displays a list of the types currently defined for that object category. In different categories, the way these lists are displayed is inconsistent.

In the people category, the type drop-down appears to be ordered alphabetically for predefined entries, but overall is not ordered alphabetically, with custom entries underlined at the bottom [see screenshot 01-type-drop-down-people-grampsw_2016-05-17.png].

In events  category, it is not necessarily ordered alphabetically even for the initial few [those within the same predefined group as the current or default type], but then the other pre-defined groups of types do NOT appear in alphabetical order, and within any one of them, MOST [all but "Legal" & "Travel"] have contents that are NOT in alphabetical order! The "Custom" category appears at the very bottom [and if it is going to be served up out of alphabetical sequence, it would at least be better if there was a separator before it], but its content IS in alphabetical order! See screenshots [02-type-drop-down-events-grampsw_2016-05-17.png for the initial display, and 03-type-drop-down-events-vocational-grampsw_2016-05-17.png for the contents of one of the predefined groups].

The current behaviour of the drop-down in the events category is a major problem!

In the places view, the drop-down is a single long list, with the "standard" types in alphabetical order at the top, and the custom ones following in a new alphabetical list at the bottom, with no marker of the change. Again, if they are going to be displayed other than in a continuous alphabetical sequence, it would be better if there was a separator between the two lists. See screenshot [04-type-drop-down-places-grampsw-2016-05-17].

In the repository & notes views, the drop-down is in alphabetical order.

Summary: Relationship view link names appear and disappear
Description:
Loaded 5.0 beta1 on High Sierra. Opened the Relationships tab and saw a full normal view. I then moved my cursor up and down within the scrolling area where Parents and Family are displayed. As I move up and down over the highlighted names, some times the name (which is a link) turns invisible. If I move the cursor over it again, it reappears. It is only the names that disappear. It seems random whether a name has this problem. 

It is only the Relationship tab that has this issue. I suspect because the names are links.
Steps To Reproduce: Load on MacOS. Open Relataionship tab with a family that has many names (mother, father, spouse children). Now move the cursor up and down over the names, where the cursor changes from normal to highlighting the link.

Summary: Text disappears in Relationship view
Description:
Frequently, text (usually a person's name) disappears from the window in Relationship view. See "Screen Shot 2019-04-07 at 7.01.19 PM.png".
Moving the mouse over the affected area causes it to redraw. See "Screen Shot 2019-04-07 at 7.01.41 PM.png". 

Unfortunately, I do not know when the problem appeared (e.g., if it started with 5.x or was in 4.x). I believe it started with 5.x, but there were larger issues at the time on macOS, so I never mentioned this particular problem.
Steps To Reproduce: Working in the Relationship view, it's not hard to run into this. One consistent thing is that it always seems to show up when items are newly drawn (e.g., you clicked on a person forcing a screen change or were scrolling because there are many Siblings/Children in the list. Often, it's not just one name that's missing, but several—not necessarily in a row, though.
Additional Information:
Since I happen to have been running gramps from the console (for another issue), I notice that when text disappears, this type of message is produced:
(Gramps.app:82875): Gtk-CRITICAL **: 19:01:01.263: gtk_widget_draw: assertion '!widget->priv->alloc_needed' failed
Such a message does seem to be rather consistent, even if it is not directly related, that I thought it worth mentioning.

This may be Port for Mac related, but as I am not running on Linux or Windows, so I don't know.

Summary: Children menus to browse tree may be NOT working in "Pedigree view"
Description: Children menu to browse tree from Charts category may be NOT working. The functionality of this menu depends from the way you access it.
Steps To Reproduce:
Open a tree.
Go to Charts category and browse a tree by clicking on the arrow pointing to children.
Go to a person having more than one child and click the arrow in order to get the menu with the list of children.
Now, take care: once you click the arrow showing the menu, move the mouse going DIRECTLY into the menu WITHOUT WALKING OUT FROM IT, NOT EVEN BEFORE REACHING IT.
The menu becomes NOT accessible. I mean: the software does not allow you to make a selection because no item (no child) responds to the mouse click. Items don't become highlighted when the mouse hover them.

In the attached picture I draw the "correct mouse path" and the wrong one.
The correct mouse path is the path leading to the error. It goes DIRECTLY from the arrow to the menu. The wrong one is the path that does not lead to the error. Of course the path that I called "wrong" is the one that make the menu working.
Additional Information: Workaround: to have the menu responding to mouse hover get out from the menu of children with the mouse and get into it again. This fixes the problem here.
