Summary: Converting gramps.pot
Description:
I was using Poedit (as recommended by Mirko) to work on my lang.po.  I ran into a lot of problem with the string formats that we have in the current gramps.pot (branch maintenance/gramps50).  Poedit does not recognize these formats as to be excluded from its pre-translation process.  There are currently over 2000+  that need attention in order to comply with Python 3.5++.  

I spoke to Paul; and he suggested I take the first stab at fixing it.  I dont have update access to gramps.git to do a normal Pull Request, so he recommended that I create a bug and send gramps.pot along with additional info on the conversions that I did.  Beside, this works  better for me I do need a few more pairs of eyes to check what I am doing.  I am not ready to take off my training wheel yet ;(

As I mentioned to Paul, I will convert only where I feel safe to do so, such as taking the simple form %(text)s, the same with d and i,  That all will be converted to {text}.  More complicated forms such as with other operator, or using width, justification, and fancy manipulations will be left alone, and flagged on the log as a manual [TODO].  In fact there 32 of them.

For this task, I coded a small python program.  This program emits a log wheteher any candidates %(text) can be fixed [DONE] or not (as [TODO]), based on its specified operator.  Also, the program produces a new gramps.pot (in a different folder, of course)..

Here are the attached files:
. new gramps.pot
. a text file gramp_POT_conversion.log for your reading
. a git diff output (gramps.diff) in color that is supposed to be displayable in color on notepad++, however it didnt work for me.  It on the other hand, displays text in color if you use the DOS command TYPE (or unix cat) , but it went too fast to be any practical.   I included it just in case some may know how to use it.  The git command I used was: git diff --color gramps.pot >gramps.diff

Please let me know if you see somethings not right, or have any questions on this bug report.   Thanks.
dave
Steps To Reproduce: see above
Additional Information: Seems I have a hard time uploading the mentioned files.  I will do it right after I hit the submit button ;)

Summary: GV docgen: JPG and PNG, Thumbnail images not within the graph nodes at large DPI
Description:
In an older Version of Gramps, I've created a Relationship Graph and I've included thumnail images, that were placed within a box. 

In the actual Version, the thumbnail images are placed half-in and half-out of the box. 

I've tried several settings, but I get still a poor relationship graph (see image attached).
Steps To Reproduce:
Paper Options: 
* Self defined, 60 cm width, 40 cm hight, 
* Orientation: Portrait

Report Options: entire database
* Include Birth, Marriage and Death dates
* Use place when no date
* Include IDs 
* Include thumbnail images of people

Graph Style: Color fill 
* Arrowhead direction: Descendants <- Ancestors, 
* Show family nodes

GraphViz Layout
* Font family: True Type/ FreeSans 
* Font size: 12 
* Graph Direction: Vertical (top to bottom)
* Number of Horizontal Pages: 1 
* Number of Vertical Pages: 1

GraphViz Options: 
* Aspect Ratio: Minimal Size (as well as ) Fill the given area
* DPI: 300
* Node Spacing: 1 
* Rank Spacing: 0,2

Note
* Note to add to the graph: 17. November 2012
* Note Location : bottom of the page
* Note size: 8
Additional Information:
I've found out a workaround:
a) Create a Relationship Graph and save it as SVG.
b) Then, start GIMP and let GIMP render your SVG with your large size properties for a poster.
c) Save Image as PNG or JPG and then sent it to your photographic laboratory. 


https://gramps-project.org/wiki/index.php/Gramps_5.1_Wiki_Manual_-_Reports_-_part_5#Relationship_Graph

Summary: [GTK]Sort arrow in column header points in the unexpected way on MacOS and Windows.
Description:
At the risk of stepping into some long-running OS dispute, I want to point out that for MacOS and Windows users, the little arrows indicating sort order by the column title point the other way from that used by those OSes. For example, an alphabetic sort with A at the top and Z and the bottom of the column gets an up arrow in MacOS/Windows, but Gramps shows a down.

I'm a long time Mac user and a Windows user when forced. Not much linux or other UNIX experience, at least with a GUI. From some internet searching, it seems like Gramps may be using the convention found in UNIX GUIs.

An option to reverse this would be nice.

I attach some fragmentary screen shots showing the difference between the MacOS Finder and Gramps. In both cases the sorting is ascending (a at the top).

Summary: Trees of Dynamic Web Report strip hyphen from hyphenated names
Description:
The trees generated in the Dynamic Web Report strip out any hyphen that exists within a given name (as in "Jan-Åke") or within a surname (as in "HAMILTON-SMITH"). The hyphen gets replaced with a space.

This only appears to happen in the DWR tree. Other instances of the name within any of the various indexes or tabs of the DWR display the hyphen as originally entered in the gramps name editor, and even the mouse-over display of information in the DWR tree view includes the hyphen.

The DWR search function seems to find the name either way (entering a hyphen, or entering a space, as in "Jan-Åke" or "Jan Åke", when the underlying name is "Jan-Åke").

It is confusing for some instances of a name to appear with spaces, and others with hyphens. The hyphen is an allowed character in a name in Gramps, within personal names in many jurisdictions, and in various international schemas relating to personal names for identity management, so Gramps reports should display it, and do so consistently.

The mechanism provided in the DWR for tree pages to generate SVG code is an extremely useful way of producing scalable printable charts from Gramps, but needs to display names as actually entered within Gramps.
Steps To Reproduce:
1. Generate a DWR from a database which contains some example names containing hyphens
2. Navigate within the DWR to any example individual with a hyphen within either the given name field, or the surname field (or even both), noting the display of the hyphen(s) within the indexes and text tabs while doing so.
3. Having selected a single individual with a hyphenated name, note the display of hyphen(s) within the text on screen (in both the heading, and the "birth name" fields), click on the tree tab
4. Note that the name in the coloured box displays without hyphen (i.e. space in place of the hyphen)
5. Move the cursor over the box containing the individual's name, noting that the hyphen within the name is shown in the mouse-over display
Additional Information: Attached screenshots show two instances of the individual Joe ST-PIERRE (I01766) from the Gramps example database (a) text on his base DWR page, displaying hyphen in two places (b) his box on the tree page, displaying space instead of hyphen, also with mouseover displaying hyphen

Summary: incorrect display of unique surnames in TopSurnames and Statistics gramplets for Russian patronymic 'surnames'
Description: As far as I understand from some past conversations in the users mailing list archive, the recommended way to handle Russian patronymic names is to use non-primary surnames of origin 'patronymic'. This results in some ugliness in the TopSurnames and Statistics gramplets, which now display and count, say, "Иванов Петрович", "Иванов Сергеевич", "Иванов Андреевич" as separate surnames, while according to the Russian language conventions all these represent only one surname "Иванов".
Steps To Reproduce:
1. Add a new person
2. Type "Иванов" into the Surname field
3. Press the "Use Multiple Surnames" button
4. Press the "Add" button
5. Type "Петрович" into the "Surname" column and select "Patronymic" in the "Origin" column
6. Press "Ok"
7. Notice that "Иванов Сергеевич, 100% (1)" is displayed in the TopSurnames gramplet
8. Notice that "Иванов Сергеевич" appear in the list of "Unique Surnames" accessible from the Statistics gramplet
9. Repeat steps 1-8 with "Сергеевич" and "Андреевич" in step 5
10. Notice that both gramplets now displays three 'unique' surnames

Summary: surname-related plugins do not take global surname mapping into account
Description:
TopSurnamesGramplet and StatsGramplet, FilterByName and SameSurnames quick views do not take global surname mapping into account when counting and diplaying surnames. 

In fact, TopSurnamesGramplet and FilterByName internally use Name.get_group_name(), taking account of local overrides, whereas StatsGramplet and SameSurnames just use Name.get_surname(), ignoring both local and global overrides.
Steps To Reproduce:
Either 
(a) look into the code, or

(b) run Gramps, add two persons, with surnames Smith and Jones, edit Jones's surname, set its "Group As" property to "Smith" and choose to make it global ("Group All"), then check out TopSurnamesGramplet and StatsGramplet: Smith and Jones are displayed separately
Additional Information: The attached patches fixes the issue by defining function get_surname() in Utils which applies overrides in the proper order, and using this function in all four cases.

The names that were originally entered with multiple surnames now show up as single surnames, which is understandable, but the prefixes of all names end up in a single prefix, with comma's between them, and the same seem to happen to the surnames too, resulting in a full name that does not equal the original.
Additional Information:
According to the written standard, the use of comma's on export is right, but I have yet to see another program that writes those when multiple surnames or prefixes are found. Moreover, the comma's added on export are not interpreted as list separators on import in Gramps itself.

The attached file was produced by Gramps 3.4.3

This makes it very difficult to select an accurate sub-region without selecting, saving, selecting, saving, etc.  The previous version I used (4.0.3 I think) zoomed in on the sub-region allowing for better support for minor adjustments.

Create new
select media object
select small sub-region

Summary: Descendant Lines confusing when cousins marry
Description:
Hello,

In the case where 1st cousins marry, in this report the "line" goes through the woman, not the man.  In patronymic societies like ours -- and just about all others -- I think that this is a wrong strategy, and that the line should go through the man.

I'm uploading a PDF which shows this action: Riegle, Frank D (I0358) married Alta Herr (I0361) in about 1896.  Their children are all Riegles, but are under the Herr side of the tree.
Additional Information: https://gramps-project.org/wiki/index.php?title=Gramps_4.1_Wiki_Manual_-_Reports_-_part_6#Detailed_Descendant_Report

Summary: Duplication in graphical reports when first cousins marry
Description: When first cousins marry, the graphical (descendant) reports all seem to duplicate the descendants of the married couple. That is: these reports all give the complete descendant tree for X married to Y, and then again for Y married to X. To resolve this, apart from eliminating the duplication, the tree structure can require crossing "branches" ....
Steps To Reproduce: Any data file with married first cousins will reproduce this behaviour. My own data file is very large, but I could probably provide a "demonstration" file with some effort. Else, I can provide PDF of a graphical report of that part of my file that shows the problem.
Additional Information: I previously reported this as a problem in DenominoViso .... but I now realise that it is present in all the graphical descendant trees. Presumably, they all use the same basic code.

Summary: [Place Reference Editor]No field has the focus when used this is inconsistent Form Input Behavior Adding a Place Object
Description: If I select a Place in the Places Category, then click the Add Place button on the Tool Bar (Blue Plus Sign), it opens a form window titled New Place. When the form opens the focus is given to the Name field so I can just start typing. Very considerate. Now, in the process of cleaning up a huge import I need to "add" a Place object 'above' the Place in the Place window. So, I double click on the selected Place and an editing window opens. I then select the Add Place button on the Enclosed By button, which opens a window titled Place Reference Editor. It is very similar to the New Place form with one important, operational difference: No field has the focus. I am now required to use an extra mouse click to get the focus into the Name field to start typing. Entering five a day would be no big deal, however, cleaning up an import amounts to hundreds, if not thousands a day. Would be nice to have the Name field get the focus in this situation. Best, Paul
Steps To Reproduce:
1. Open a Place for editing.

2. Click + on Enclosed By Tab.

3. Opened form has no field selected.
Additional Information:
My first gripe. I'm new here, so please be easy, for know.

GrampsAIO64-5.0.1-1

Windows 10, 64 Bit 

https://gramps-project.org/wiki/index.php/Gramps_5.0_Wiki_Manual_-_Entering_and_editing_data:_detailed_-_part_2#Place_Reference_Editor

Summary: Relationship graph: PDF and PS outputs have no lines/colours if many people in the database
Description:
With a database of 371 people. 

When trying to make a Relationship Graph (Reports -> Graphs -> Relationship Graph) and selecting PDF (Graphviz) as an output format the PDF file is missing all the colours, borders and relationships between people in the database (see drawing.pdf top). I was able to export it as PS and then convert it to PDF with Preview (default PDF viewer on macOS) with all the colours, borders and relationships. 

With 452 poeple in the database. 

Neither PDF nore PS to PDF as described above (see drawing.pdf top) do not produce the graph with all details.
Steps To Reproduce:
Gramps 4.2.5 on macOS

Have a large database.

Select Reports -> Graphs -> Relationship Graph
Under "Report Options" select "Entire Database"
Under "Include" select "Include dates and Places"
Under "Graph Style" select "Colour fill"

On the bottom of the window under "Output format" select "PDF (Graphviz)" or PostScript (with very large database) and press OK.

The output will look similar to what can be seen in drawing.pdf top (PDF) and drawing.pdf middle (PS which is then converted to PDF with Preview).
Additional Information:
The workaround is to
1. Export the graph to SVG (Structured Vector Graphics).
2. Open it in Inkscape to work on it.
3. Open it in Firefox to save as PDF (Print -> Save as PDF on macOS or use eg PDFCreator on Windows) ... see drawing.pdf bottom
4. Use the PDF to print it out (e.g. in Acrobat Reader you can print it over multiple pages with the Poster option and choosing the scale to suit your needs)

 
-------------
https://gramps-project.org/wiki/index.php?title=Gramps_4.2_Wiki_Manual_-_Reports_-_part_5#Relationship_Graph

Summary: Copy/Paste keyboard shortcuts don't work in sidebar/filter
Description:
Copy/Paste keyboard shortcuts (CTRL+C and CTRL+V) don't work in sidebar/filter.

In views that have a filter in the sidebar, clicking CTRL+C will copy the selected item (person, family, etc) to Gramps clipboard and make the clipboard appear, even when a text field in the sidebar/filter has focus.

In these same views pressing CTRL+V will make the Gramps clipboard appear, even when a text field in the sidebar/filter has focus.

Summary: wrong export to GEDCOM with a marriage in a "Città"

As far as I can understand, this issue is an Italian language specific issue.

Export to GEDCOM appear wrong if a marriage is in a place with type = "Città"
Steps To Reproduce:
Begin a new family tree from scratch.
Add a family with an husband and a wife. (choose the names you like)
Add the marriage. Choose the date you like for the wedding BUT be sure to give it a place.
As a place choose a name from your fantasy and mark it as "Città". (Città in Italian is like Town in English)
Export to gedcom.

Go back to your family tree.
Change the type of place from "città" to "Town"
Export again to to gedcom.

Compare the two exported files.
They are different and the first is wrong.

Attached are my two exported files.

Summary: Rework pruning in FamilyLines
Description: I have a largish family tree where the main feature is that several modern people share a couple of 17th century ancestors. In order to show this situation graphically, I use FamilyLines. For the graph to not be totally unreadable, I need to use the "remove extra people" option.(option called: remove extra people and families in Gramps 3.4)("Try to remove extra people and families" is in the "Report Options" tab of the options dialog in Gramps 4.2.x) I've found that this option didn't work particularly well for my case: 1) Families did not get removed where they should have been as they were only related to one of the people of interest. 2) Families got removed althought they were related to more than one person of interest. So, I've reworked this part of the code to only make use of relationships. While it does now work great for my use case, it is also extremely slow as for each person, the family tree and the relationship to each person of interest gets calculated. This change also keeps all common ancestors, not only the first. I am not sure if I like this. I wonder if there is a way to store a precalculated familytree and to answer the question "is the currently selected person related to more than one of the people of interest" more efficiently. Attached is a patch of my current(2013) work. Another desired feature would be to add coloured paths between the people of interest and also to put indicators for "a family was removed here".
Additional Information:
https://gramps-project.org/wiki/index.php?title=Gramps_3.4_Wiki_Manual_-_Reports_-_part_5#Family_Lines_Graph

https://gramps-project.org/wiki/index.php?title=Gramps_4.2_Wiki_Manual_-_Reports_-_part_5#Family_Lines_Graph

https://github.com/gramps-project/gramps/blob/master/gramps/plugins/graph/gvfamilylines.py

Summary: "Event" type not taken into account. when "Preferred Name" is not a "Birth" event
Description:

Using of the preffered name is ambiguous if they divorced later and the wife married again. The correct solution is: using the birth name.
Steps To Reproduce:

The correct sentence would be: "He married White, Agnes on 2000-02-03."

Summary: Text formatted as italic in a citation note appears as normal when Family Sheet report is run.
Description: If I format text as italic in a citation note, it appears as normal (non-italic) when Family Sheet report is run.
Steps To Reproduce: Add a citation.  Add a citation note.  Select note and select the italic font.  Use citation is a family. Run the Family Sheet report.  Check appearance of the citation.  It is not in italic.
Additional Information:
FamilySheet addon

https://gramps-project.org/wiki/index.php?title=Family_Sheet

Summary: Main Gramps windows set to top window id irrespective of any other app running on desktop
Description:
The following event occurs at random, during the addition of children to a relationship.

Several children could be entered before the problem occurs.

Problem: After clicking the add child (+) button in relationship view or in family edit dialogue, the edit person dialogue disappears behind the main gramps window. The main gramps window is now fixed as the top window of the display, irrespective of any other application running on the desktop.
Steps To Reproduce:
As mentioned above, there is a randomness element to the fault.
It usually happens when I am adding a new person object or editing an existing person pbject. via relationship view or family edit with relationship view.

Summary: fanchartview - Birth-event without date hides baptism-event with date
Description:
Fanchart makes use of the function get_age to determine the coloring. 
Often, a baptism record is available, while a birth record is not. In this case, get_age will fall back (correctly) to the baptism record to calculate the age.

However, in some cases the birth location is also known, just not the date. The creation of a birth record with only location in Gramps however 'hides' in these cases the baptism record for the get_age function, due to which no age can be calculated.
Steps To Reproduce: Create a person with a baptism record with date, and a death record with date. Age should be colored in fanchartview. Addition of birth record with only location leads to removal of color.
Additional Information:
Attached a patch to fix this. It will work for all functions calling get_age. Should also be applicable to older versions of Gramps.

Calls of e.g. get_birth_of_fallback in pedigreeview might also benefit from requiring a date, I did not adapt those yet. 


-------------------------
https://github.com/gramps-project/gramps/blob/master/gramps/gen/utils/db.py

Summary: Events with estimated dates
Description:
I have many events in my tree with estimated dates. Using the filter sidebar, in the Events view, I typed "est" into the date field. Only 13 events are found, although there are many more events with estimated dates in the tree.

All the events selected do have estimated dates.

I can't see any difference between the events selected and events missed.

Experimenting more, I found that searching for "esti" "estim" etc only finds 6 events.
Steps To Reproduce: see above
Additional Information: Not sure if this is a bug, or if dates are not intended to be searchable in this way.

Summary: [Addon] Do not create a right path for locale with ISO3166_code_length > 2 (ISO639)
Description: Some locales like 'pt_XX' or 'zh_CN' do not have a right path for storing related 'addon.mo' file (../LC_MESSAGES/..) e.g. 'ChangeGivenNames/locale/BR/LC_MESSAGES/addon.mo' See ~33754 Attached patch for changing: length= len(po) locale = po[length-11:length-9] to something like: locale = os.path.basename(po[:-3])[:-6]

Summary: Editor Windows open in the background and Gramps blocks the whole desktop
Description: When Gramps is maximized and you work with an other application like LibreOffice and you click on an edit button in Gramps without make Gramps the active application before, Gramps will block the whole desktop.
Steps To Reproduce:
Under Windows
- open Gramps maximized on the whole screen
- go into a view with editor buttons
- open a second application so that you can still see the editor buttons in Gramps
- go with the mouse cursor over a editor button in Gramps (still Gramps maximized and not active application)
- wait till the help text for the button is displayed
- click on the edit button
- now the Editor ill open behind the Gramps main Window and the main Window will fill the whole screen (except status bar)
- the only way to work with the other applications is to minimize Gramps over the status bar.
- you can not close Gramps because the visible main Window of Gramps is not the active Window of Gramps


when you don't have Gramps in the full size mode the editor window will also open in the background when you click after the help text for the button appeared, but then you can move the main window and the editor will come into the foreground.

Summary: Changing Display Name format should trigger a sort-order rebuild
Description: If you change the Display Name format in Preferences, it does refresh screen with new display, but doesn't change the sort order (which it should).
Steps To Reproduce:
1. go to people flat view
2. change display name in preference
3. view updates, but sort order does not
4. if you re-open the database, sort-order is rebuilt based on display name

Summary: The family Events view does not refresh when participants change details
Description: The family Events view does not refresh when participants change details.
Steps To Reproduce:
Open the attached gramps file.
It only counts 2 persons and a family.
Go to the "Families" category and click twice the family F0000
At the bottom of the family detail window, display the Events view.
You will found three events: one marriage and two births equipped with "Main participants" names.

In the upper side of the window, select the editing icon close to the Father/partner1 name and edit him.
Change his name from aaa to xxx and save by pressing the "Ok" button.

Once you are back to the family window, look at the Events view.
You will still find the OLD name of the "Father - 1" in the "Main participants" column.

Summary: New citation forced after filtering citations/sources
Description:
I can add an existing citation to a person's "Source Citations" by selecting a source within the newly opened "Select Source or Citation" window. Clicking the little triangle next to it drops down a list of existing citations, which I can select to add to the current person. However, if I use the search/filter at the top of the "Select Source or Citation" window to limit the sources shown I only see the sources themselves without the option to drop down and select individual citations that belong to these sources themselves (the little triangle is missing). Selecting a source this way automatically causes a new citation to be created. 

This means I can only add an existing citation if I do not use the search/filter function. If I do I always create a new citation by default. I figure this is a GUI problem?

Not critical, but annoying.

Thanks for the effort - this is a great program!
Steps To Reproduce:
1) Open an individual person record (double-click for separate window).
2) Go to Source Citations tab.
3) Click on "Add Existing Citation..."
4) In the newly opened window with the list of sources/citations use the filter at the top to search for something that limits the number of sources/citations shown and click on "find".
5) The filtered list shown consists only of sources - no linked citations to these sources are visible/selectable any longer (as opposed to before using the filter function).
6) Selecting a source will automatically generate a new citation.

Summary: People View, Citations gramplet, lists irrelevant citations
Description:
In People View/Person View, the Citations gramplet is one of the default gramplets present in the bottombar.

For some, but not all, people in my tree, too many citations are listed. In addition to citations added to the person, citations for the peron's events, citations for family events, etc, there are also citations which have no connection with the selected person.

As far as I can see, the citations are always of sources for which a citation is attached to the person (or event, family, etc). i.e. incorrect citations are listed, but never citations of the incorrect sources.

Also, the same citation may be listed several times, but perhaps this is intended (for example, if the same citation is attached to a person's birth and baptism).

I have uploaded a small tree, "test tree.gramps" to illustrate.
Steps To Reproduce: * Import and open "test tree.gramps" * look at Joe Bloggs in that tree, * View the citations tab in the bottom bar and you will see that the "Birth Index Source" citation for Jean Smith is listed. I think this happens because both citations of "Birth Index Source" are added to the place "Stow on the Wold", where Joe Bloggs was born.
Additional Information:
GRAMPS: 3.4.6-1 
Python: 2.7.2 (default, Jun 12 2011, 14:24:46) [... 
BSDDB: 4.8.4.1 (4, 7, 25) 
LANG: en_GB.UTF-8
OS: win32
Distribution:
